title: browallia amicora
intro: Browallia amicora, also known as the 'Blue-eyed Mary' or 'Mexican browallia', is a flowering plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its blue or purple flowers with white centers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Browallia amicora include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing respiratory irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
